countries.name: New Zealand
countries.footnote: World-first mandatory climate disclosure (FMC Act, 2024). NABERSNZ aligned with Australia. Provides a regulatory template for AP markets despite smaller market size.

countries.name: Malaysia
countries.footnote: Growing ASEAN RE investment market. Bursa Malaysia ISSB-aligned reporting now mandatory. GBI adoption increasing.

countries.name: Indonesia
countries.footnote: Largest ASEAN economy. Regulatory depth nascent but market scale warrants monitoring. OJK sustainable finance roadmap in force.

countries.name: Thailand
countries.footnote: Established ASEAN commercial RE market. TREES rating, mandatory building energy code for designated buildings, SEC One Report for listed companies.

countries.name: India
countries.footnote: Major and growing RE investment market. SEBI BRSR mandatory for top 1,000 listed (with assurance). ECBC mandatory in adopting states. One of the few AP markets requiring ESG assurance.

countries.name: UK
countries.footnote: International comparator. Leading on MEES (mandatory building energy performance for lettings), TCFD-mandated disclosure, Biodiversity Net Gain. Benchmark for AP markets.

countries.name: France
countries.footnote: International comparator. France-specific regulations (RE2020, Décret Tertiaire) are often more advanced than EU-wide requirements. Leads globally on whole-life carbon caps and mandatory energy reduction pathways.

countries.name: EU
countries.footnote: EU-level directives and regulations (CSRD, EPBD, Nature Restoration) that apply across member states. Shown separately from France because France often exceeds EU minimums and has additional national requirements.

countries.name: US
countries.footnote: International comparator. Federal policy stalled (SEC rule stayed), but city/state-level building performance standards (NYC LL97, WA CBPS) are globally significant. Demonstrates fragmented-federal pattern.

countries.name: Canada
countries.footnote: International comparator. Provincial energy step codes (BC) and federal green buildings strategy. CSSB adopting ISSB. Similar federal/provincial structure to Australia.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.footnote: International comparator. World leader in embodied carbon regulation — MPG (Milieuprestatie Gebouwen) mandatory for all new buildings >100m² since 2013, tightened 2023. Also leads on circular economy transition. Key reference for EC and WC themes.

countries.name: Denmark
countries.footnote: International comparator. Mandatory whole-life carbon calculation under BR18 for new buildings, with tightening performance limits. Progressive building regulation; relevant for EC theme.

countries.name: Finland
countries.footnote: International comparator. Mandatory low-carbon building regulation in force from April 2025 — one of first countries to mandate embodied carbon performance limits for new construction. Relevant for EC theme.
